World.headline: US-Iran agree to two-week ceasefire, with conditions on Hormuz opening and diplomacy
World.summary: US President Trump announced a two-week pause on military strikes against Iran, contingent on the immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and diplomatic engagement. While a narrow window for talks has opened, the situation remains volatile due to ongoing military activity. Iran has proposed a 10-point peace framework, but discrepancies highlight the fragility of the de-escalation efforts.
key_points: Trump announced a two-week pause on military strikes against Iran.
Cessation of hostilities is conditional on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz and engaging diplomatically.
A window for talks has opened, but the situation remains fragile.
Iran proposed a 10-point peace framework with ongoing discrepancies.

World.headline: Netanyahu: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but not Hezbollah truce in Lebanon
World.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu stated that Israel supports the US-Iran two-week ceasefire regarding direct hostilities. However, he emphasized that this truce does not extend to Hezbollah operations in Lebanon. This highlights Israel's distinct security concerns in the region.
key_points: Netanyahu supports the US-Iran two-week truce.
The truce does not include Hezbollah in Lebanon.
Israel maintains a separate stance on regional security.

World.headline: US, Iran, and mediators discussing terms for possible 45-day ceasefire

This suggests a more substantial de-escalation effort than previously announced. Diplomatic channels are actively working towards a more prolonged period of peace and negotiation.
key_points: US, Iran, and mediators are discussing a 45-day ceasefire.
